content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

SMURR, HENRY & GELEGAN
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

HELM BUILDING
1111 FULTON MALL, SUITE 900
FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93721
AREA CODE 209
TELEPHONE 237-0207
FAX 233-2229

JOHN B. SMURR
JERRY E. HENRY
TIMOTHY R. GELEGAN
TIMOTHY V. LOGOLUSO

441 SOUTH MADERA AVENUE, SUITE C
KERMAN, CALIFORNIA 93630
AREA CODE 209
TELEPHONE 846-9356
FAX 846-5741

PLEASE REPLY TO:

FRESNO ☐
KERMAN ☒

March 26, 1990


Fair Political Practice Commission
Legal Division
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Fresno County Superior Court Case No. 389134-4
    Questions concerning the amount of civil liability
    pursuant to 91004 of the Government Code

To whom it may concern:

This letter is requesting a formal opinion from the legal
department of the FPPC concerning the total amount of civil
liability an individual could incur, upon the finding of a
failure to disclose pursuant to Government Code Section 87200 et
seq. and the finding of a violation pursuant to Government Code
Section 91004.

The California Government Code Section 91004 indicates that any
person who is found to have negligently or intentfully violated
the reporting requirements set forth for the act, could be liable
for an amount not more than the amount or value not properly
recorded. For the purpose of your opinion, let me pose a
hypothetical for your analysis.

A council member of a city owns 10 percent (100%) of
the shares of a business operating within the City.
The councilman's business receives income from company
x which also is doing business within the councilman's
city. The income received from company x during a
reporting year is $100,000.00. The councilman fails to
report the income from company x on his Schedule H Forms
for that reporting year. Pursuant to California Government
Code Section 91004, what is the appropriate amount of
potential liability for damages for the councilman?

Page 2
March 26, 1990


My understanding of the way this code section reads would allow a
judge or jury to issue damages up to the total amount of income
from company x, which would be $100,000.00. Another argument
could be that the total exposure for the councilman who failed to
report company x's income would only be $10,000.00, due to the
fact that he was only required to list company x as a source of
income of $10,000.00 and above.

I would greatly appreciate an opinion from your legal department
concerning the FPPC's position on the total amount of liability
for the violation pursuant to the above referenced hypothetical.
If the legal department is not inclined to render a formal
opinion or the commission will not render a formal opinion, I
would greatly appreciate you directing me to either case
authority or other authority which could be useful in answering
my question.

I would like to thank you for your anticipated cooperation and if
you have any questions regarding this matter, please do not
hesitate to give me a call.

Sincerely,

Timothy V. Logoluso
hmm
cc: Joe Duval

Dictated by the writer;
signed and mailed in his/nsr absence
to avoid delay.

QUESTION: s concerning the amount of civil liability pursuant to 91004 of the Government Code

To whom it may concern:

This letter is requesting a formal opinion from the legal department of the FPPC concerning the total amount of civil liability an individual could incur, upon the finding of a failure to disclose pursuant to Government Code Section 87200 et seq. and the finding of a violation pursuant to Government Code Section 91004.

The California Government Code Section 91004 indicates that any person who is found to have negligently or intentfully violated the reporting requirements set forth for the act, could be liable for an amount not more than the amount or value not properly recorded. The councilman fails to report the income from company x on his Schedule H Forms for that reporting year. Pursuant to California Government Code Section 91004, what is the appropriate amount of potential liability for damages for the councilman?

March 26, 1990

My understanding of the way this code section reads would allow a judge or jury to issue damages up to the total amount of income from company x, which would be $100,000.00. Another argument could be that the total exposure for the councilman who failed to report company x's income would only be $10,000.00, due to the fact that he was only required to list company x as a source of income of $10,000.00 and above.
